channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A humanoid robot sprints past the human half-marathon world record in Beijing race
item.description: The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.
item.content:encoded:
The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.K police investigating if attacks in London are the work of Iranian proxies
item.description: U.K. police said Sunday they are investigating whether a string of arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies, the latest of which caused minor damage on Saturday night.
item.content:encoded:
U.K. police said Sunday they are investigating whether a string of arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies, the latest of which caused minor damage on Saturday night.

(Image credit: Jamie Lashmar)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. says it seized an Iranian cargo ship, ceasefire expires soon
item.description: The two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire Wednesday. And the two sides seem far from a resolution. President Trump says the US has taken custody of an Iranian vessel.
item.content:encoded: The two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire Wednesday. And the two sides seem far from a resolution. President Trump says the US has taken custody of an Iranian vessel.
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.

(Image credit: Asghar Besharati)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
item.description: South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.
item.content:encoded:
South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: US Coast Guard spots overturned vessel near Saipan during search for missing ship
item.description: The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.
item.content:encoded:
The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Life jacket worn by a passenger who survived the Titanic auctioned off for over $900,000
item.description: A life jacket worn by a passenger on the RMS Titanic has sold at auction for 670,000 pounds, which is more than $900,000.
item.content:encoded:
A life jacket worn by a passenger on the RMS Titanic has sold at auction for 670,000 pounds, which is more than $900,000.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves 6 dead before police shot and killed the gunman
item.description: A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.
item.content:encoded:
A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.

(Image credit: Dan Bashakov)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis
item.description: A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.
item.content:encoded:
A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.

(Image credit: Nicole Tung)
item.dc:creator: Nicole Tung

item.title: Ukraine launches specialized Russian studies program to better understand the enemy
item.description: Ukraine launches political Russian studies program to better understand wartime enemy.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine launches political Russian studies program to better understand wartime enemy.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley
